## [00:00 - 02:36]

Guys, this video is just going to be a couple of minutes. We're going to go over contracts and agreements. So what I was able to do is give you guys a template here. Basically, I would have them signed in independent sales contractor agreement as well as an NDA. So we're going to put some templates in here for you guys. I just will say that one lesson I've learned just as you scale your company, look at first, you can use templates and you can just kind of model somebody else's contract. That's totally fine. Eventually, you're going to want to get to the point where you have a lawyer come in and custom write all your contracts based on where your company is located and all these different things. And just make sure it's done correctly. Because I found out at a certain point that we had all these contracts that I kind of like pieced together from other businesses I knew and business owners. And I was kind of using theirs. And I had somebody look at ours and they just said, literally if anything happened between any team member, none of these contracts would hold up in court. So it's like, OK, great. So we just have these fake contracts. So eventually, you're going to want to write these for yourself or have a lawyer write these. But I think for now, it is totally fine to hire your first couple of people just really quickly, just using kind of like a template. So at least there's some expectations there. And then as you grow, you can just tell them, hey, we brought in a lawyer. We're here with our contracts. So what we're going to do is we're rolling everything out. We're having everybody sign a new agreement. And nobody will have an issue with that. Everybody will do it. So basically, as you can see here, you can read this. It's pretty self-explanatory. Some of these things here, like for instance, you may want to just change this to reflect your actual comp structure, how you're going to do payroll, custom payment plans, different things like this. So you can just go through all of this, read it, and you'll see the areas that you obviously want to change for your offer. And then there's also an NDA. Read through this. You'll see there's certain things you want to change for your offer. I tried to make all of the basic stuff really obvious, so all caps, your name, your legal name, et cetera, your state, your state. So a lot of this stuff you should be able to kind of read through and it should stick out to you. But you can use these for now. I do recommend, though, once you get rolling and you have a little bit of cash to spend on this type of stuff, that I would hire an attorney and just have them rewrite your contracts all in all. I'll probably put a recommendation below this video of the person we used. So you're more than welcome to reach out to him if you would like. So that's it for this training, guys.
